description: Database Designer orchestrates a pipeline: Requirements Analyst → Schema Designer → show prototype to user → user approves → Schema Validator → Builder. terminateAfter is false for Analyst and Designer (you react to their results), true for Validator only (its output goes directly to the user).

example_1_new_entity_request.description: User describes a new entity. Call Requirements Analyst immediately — do not ask questions yourself.

subAgent.name: Database Requirements Analyst
subAgent.message: Gather functional requirements for a new entity. User wants: 'track project milestones — checkpoints with a title, due date, and status'. Write FINAL if you have enough info, or DRAFT with up to 3 questions if you need clarification. Return when done.

example_2_analyst_returned_draft.description: Requirements Analyst returned with DRAFT — it has questions. Extract them from FunctionalRequirements and show them to the user as a form. Do not call any sub-agent yet.
example_2_analyst_returned_draft.message: Before I design the schema, I have a few questions:

questions.label: Should milestones have a status (e.g. Active, Completed, Cancelled) or just a done/not-done flag?
Should each milestone belong to a project? If so, is Projects already an entity in your system?
Do you want to assign milestones to a specific team member (link to Users)?

example_3_user_answered_draft_questions.description: User answered the draft questions. Call Requirements Analyst again with their answers so it can finalize FunctionalRequirements.

subAgent.name: Database Requirements Analyst
subAgent.message: Update FunctionalRequirements with the user's answers. User said: 'Yes, status: Active/Completed/Cancelled. Link to Projects entity. Assign to a team member via Users.' Finalize to FINAL mode — we have enough to design. Return when done.

example_4_requirements_final_call_design.description: FunctionalRequirements is complete (no DRAFT), SchemaDesign is not yet in payload. Call Schema Designer. It will write SchemaDesign to the payload and return — you then show the prototype.

subAgent.name: Database Schema Designer
subAgent.message: Design the schema based on FunctionalRequirements in the payload. Research any FK targets first using Database Research Agent. Write SchemaDesign (Prototype + TableDefinition + ModificationType) to payload and return Success. Do NOT show the prototype to the user — the parent agent will present it.

example_5_designer_returned_show_prototy.description: Schema Designer returned — SchemaDesign is now in the payload. YOU show SchemaDesign.Description (if present), SchemaDesign.Prototype, and SchemaDesign.ERDMermaid (if present, as a ```mermaid block) to the user with the approval buttons. When the user clicks 'Looks good — create it', the framework will send 'Does this design look right?: create_now' as the next user message.
example_5_designer_returned_show_prototy.message:
📁 __mj_UDT.ProjectMilestones — new entity

Here's the proposed design for **Project Milestones**:

Project Milestones tracks key checkpoints in projects with status, due dates, and optional assignment to team members.

| Column | SQL Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| ProjectID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | ✅ Yes | — | FK → Projects.ID |
| AssignedUserID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | No | — | FK → __mj.User.ID |
| Title | NVARCHAR(200) | ✅ Yes | — | Milestone display name |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Active' | Lifecycle status: Active, Completed, Cancelled |
| DueDate | DATE | No | — | Target completion date |
| Notes | NVARCHAR(MAX) | No | — | Optional notes about this milestone |

```mermaid
erDiagram
    ProjectMilestones {
        UNIQUEIDENTIFIER ProjectID FK
        UNIQUEIDENTIFIER AssignedUserID FK
        NVARCHAR Title
        NVARCHAR Status
        DATE DueDate
        NVARCHAR Notes
    }

    Projects ||--o{ ProjectMilestones : ""
    User ||--o{ ProjectMilestones : ""
```

Auto-managed by CodeGen (do not add): `ID` · `__mj_CreatedAt` · `__mj_UpdatedAt`

example_6_user_clicked_create_now.description: The last user message is 'Does this design look right?: create_now' — the framework formats button clicks as '[label]: [value]', so create_now means the user approved. Call Schema Validator immediately. No message, no responseForm, no confirmation — just the sub-agent call. terminateAfter MUST be false — the parent must react to ValidationResult (success → route to Builder; failure → report errors).

subAgent.name: Database Schema Validator
subAgent.message: Validate the TableDefinition in SchemaDesign.TableDefinition. Check authorization, schema blocklist, naming conflicts, and structural validity. Return ValidationResult.

example_7_user_wants_schema_change.description: User asked to change the design ('add a Notes field'). Call Schema Designer again with the change appended. After it returns, show the updated prototype.

subAgent.name: Database Schema Designer
subAgent.message: Design the schema based on FunctionalRequirements in the payload. Research any FK targets first using Database Research Agent. Write SchemaDesign (Prototype + TableDefinition + ModificationType) to payload and return Success. Do NOT show the prototype to the user — the parent agent will present it. Also apply this change from the user: add a Notes column (long text, optional).

example_8_build_success.description: DatabaseDesignerResult is in the payload — entity was created successfully. Report it to the user and set taskComplete: true.
example_8_build_success.message:
Your new entity is live!

- **Entity Name**: Project Milestones
- **Table**: `__mj_UDT.ProjectMilestones`
- **Columns**: 4 custom + 3 auto-managed (ID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt)

You can start creating records immediately via the API or Explorer UI.

example_10_subagent_mode_entry.description: payload.mode === 'subagent' and callerContext.tableSpec is present but SchemaDesign is absent. Intercept 1 fires: bypass Requirements Analyst entirely and route directly to Schema Designer with the tableSpec embedded in the message.

subAgent.name: Database Schema Designer
subAgent.message:
Subagent mode — called by Planning Designer Agent.

Specification:
```json
{"name": "Customer Orders", "description": "Tracks orders placed by customers", "schemaName": "__mj_UDT"}
```

Design the schema for this entity. Skip DB research (Step 1) — the calling agent has already determined this is a new entity. Write SchemaDesign (Prototype + TableDefinition + ModificationType=create) to the payload and return Success.

example_11_subagent_confirmed_by_parent.description: payload.mode === 'subagent', callerContext.subagentConfirmedByParent === true, and SchemaDesign is absent. The calling agent already obtained user approval. The message includes 'User approval was already obtained' — Schema Designer should skip the approval step and proceed directly.

subAgent.name: Database Schema Designer
subAgent.message:
Subagent mode — called by Planning Designer Agent.

Specification:
```json
{"name": "Customer Orders", "description": "Tracks orders placed by customers"}
```

User approval was already obtained by the calling agent — do not ask for confirmation. Write SchemaDesign and return Success immediately.

example_12_validation_passed_route_to_bu.description: ValidationResult.Valid === true is in the payload and DatabaseDesignerResult is absent. Intercept 3 fires automatically — route to Schema Builder. No user interaction needed.

subAgent.name: Database Schema Builder
subAgent.message: Build the entity defined in SchemaDesign.TableDefinition. The schema has passed validation. Create the table, run the migration pipeline, and write DatabaseDesignerResult to the payload.

example_9_validation_failure.description: ValidationResult is in the payload and validation failed. Explain the problem and offer options.
example_9_validation_failure.message:
There's a problem with the schema:

**Error**: An entity named `Project Milestones` already exists in `__mj_UDT`.

Would you like to use a different name, or modify the existing entity instead?
